name: MCAS Yuma
tagline: A Marine Corps F-35 air station and the home of Marine aviation weapons and tactics training, in the Arizona desert.
_note: Marine Corps Air Station Yuma (Yuma, AZ; Sonoran Desert; Arizona does not observe DST -> America/Phoenix). CONUS full template (BAH MHA AZ016 Yuma; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). F-35B operations + MAWTS-1 (Marine Aviation Weapons and Tactics Squadron, runs the WTI course). Distinct from Army's Yuma Proving Ground (already in app), same city. Medical: branch clinic. Facts verified from official sources (yuma.marines.mil, TRICARE, DeCA/MCX, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Arizona is TRICARE West. Affordable; very hot summers. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 85365 (Yuma).

data.Home of: MAWTS-1, which runs the Weapons and Tactics Instructor (WTI) course
data.Climate: Hot, dry desert with abundant flying weather
data.Note: Arizona does not observe daylight saving time

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Marine Corps Air Station Yuma is a key Marine aviation base, operating the F-35B and home to MAWTS-1, which runs the elite Weapons and Tactics Instructor (WTI) course. Its clear desert skies make it ideal for flight operations year-round.
It's in Yuma in southwestern Arizona's Sonoran Desert, an affordable area known for sunshine and heat, with the Colorado River and California's coast within driving distance.

data.subtitle: Yuma is affordable and sunny, with extreme summer heat. The main choice is which part of town fits your commute and schools, with on-base housing also available.
intro: Yuma's cost of living is low, so BAH stretches well. On-base housing is available; many also live off base around the city.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools across Yuma and the Foothills, with the desert heat the main lifestyle factor.

recommendations.advice: Central and south Yuma keep the commute short.
The Foothills and newer east-side neighborhoods are popular with families.
On-base housing is available and convenient; weigh it against the affordable local market.
Summers are extreme; budget for cooling and enjoy the great fall, winter, and spring.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official MCAS Yuma site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: MCAS Yuma has privatized on-base family housing across several neighborhoods.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and waitlists.

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Yuma's elementary districts and the Yuma Union High School District serve the area; confirm the right district for your address.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: Outpatient primary and some specialty care on base; inpatient, emergency, and complex care via the Yuma network (and, for some needs, San Diego or Phoenix)

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and Yuma's affordable market makes buying a real option for many families.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your orders feel uncertain.
You want time to learn the area before committing.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing when you PCS out.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The affordable Yuma market fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a neighborhood and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, but Yuma's affordability makes buying worth a look for longer tours. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, weigh your tour length, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your command and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Check on-base availability and get the office's read on Yuma and the Foothills before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Arizona vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (your Yuma elementary district and Yuma Union HSD) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at the Yuma clinic or network providers.

data.q: What is MCAS Yuma known for?
data.a: It's a Marine F-35B air station and the home of MAWTS-1, which runs the Marine Corps' elite Weapons and Tactics Instructor (WTI) course.

data.q: Is this the same as Yuma Proving Ground?
data.a: No. Yuma Proving Ground is a separate Army test installation in the same area. MCAS Yuma is the Marine Corps air station in the city of Yuma.
On-base housing is available; many live off base around Yuma and the Foothills.
Hot, dry Sonoran Desert with extreme summers and excellent flying weather. Note Arizona doesn't observe daylight saving time.
